title: Jamestown
description: Test your knowledge of the first successful English settlement in the Americas, Jamestown.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did the Virginia Company establish Jamestown in 1607?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what a company would want from an overseas colony—what would make their investors money?

choices.content: To take valuable resources quickly and create profits for investors
To establish a stable, long-term English community in the Americas
To create a safe place for Puritans to practice their religion
To serve as a military base for attacks on Spanish colonies in the Caribbean

assessment_items.question_content:
**What major problem nearly led to the failure of Jamestown in its early years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about the 'Starving Time'—why were so many colonists unprepared to survive?

choices.content: Many colonists were unprepared for farming and suffered from starvation and disease.
Most colonists quickly returned to England after losing interest in the colony's goals.
The colonists focused only on building forts and ignored all other priorities.
Native leaders refused to allow the colonists to build any settlement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What role did John Smith play in keeping Jamestown from collapse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about Smith’s famous motto—what rule kept unprepared settlers alive?

choices.content: He imposed strict discipline and insisted everyone contribute to the colony's survival.
He discovered gold in Virginia, which allowed him to buy new supplies from England.
He married Pocahontas and secured a lasting peace with the Powhatans.
He convinced the Virginia Company to move the colony to better land.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is Pocahontas important in the history of Jamestown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how Pocahontas’s marriage helped create a temporary peace in 1614.

choices.content: She married John Rolfe, creating a temporary peace between the English and the Powhatans.
She saved Jamestown by discovering a tobacco strain that the colonists could grow.
She ruled the Powhatans after her father’s death and created a brief but strong alliance with the English settlers.
She married John Smith, creating a stable and permanent English-Powhatan alliance.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did tobacco become essential for Jamestown’s survival after 1614?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what Europeans were eager to buy from Virginia once gold was not an option.

choices.content: It was a profitable cash crop in high demand in Europe.
It grew easily without much labor in the swampy Virginia soil.
It ended the conflict with Native Americans in Virginia.
It replaced gold as a symbol of England’s military power.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did many poor Europeans agree to become indentured servants in Virginia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about why someone without money would agree to work for years in Virginia.

choices.content: They could not pay for the trip and hoped for a better life after their service.
They were forced by the English government as punishment for a crime.
They wanted to join the Virginia militia and fight in wars against the French.
They were promised riches and the right to vote after one year of service.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did tobacco’s success as a cash crop shape Virginia’s development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how tobacco linked Virginia’s economy to land expansion and bound labor systems.

choices.content: It increased demand for land and labor, driving Virginia’s growth.
It ended disputes with Native Americans over territory and trade.
It replaced gold and silver as England’s only major source of wealth.
It reduced the need for indentured servants and enslaved workers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the outcome of the Anglo-Powhatan Wars in early Virginia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what happened to Native control of land in Virginia by 1625.

choices.content: The Powhatans were killed or driven from the region, opening up land for English expansion.
The Powhatans took the fort at Jamestown and forced the English to abandon the colony permanently.
The English and Powhatans formed a lasting alliance built on mutually beneficial trade and cooperation.
The wars ended when the Virginia Company gave up its charter and moved to colonize a different region.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why do historians see Bacon’s Rebellion as a turning point in Virginia’s labor system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how Virginia’s leaders reacted to the danger of large groups of freed servants after 1676.

choices.content: It pushed colonial authorities to rely more on enslaved Africans instead of indentured servants.
It led colonial authorities to abandon tobacco farming in Virginia for several decades.
It caused colonial authorities to abolish the Virginia House of Burgesses and end local self-government.
It prompted colonial authorities to support a lasting alliance between poor colonists and enslaved Africans.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What caused Bacon’s Rebellion in Virginia in 1676?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what former indentured servants wanted most after finishing their contracts.

choices.content: Freed servants wanted land and blamed the governor for refusing to fight Native groups.
Planters lost income when tobacco prices collapsed across the Atlantic market.
Colonists revolted after the king ordered Virginia to shut down its elected assembly.
Settlers rose up after Native groups destroyed towns and crops along the Virginia coast.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why was the creation of the Virginia House of Burgesses in 1619 significant?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how the House of Burgesses set a model for later colonial self-rule.

choices.content: It established the first representative government in the English colonies.
It ended the Virginia Company’s charter and gave control of the colony to the Crown.
It gave voting rights to both indentured servants and enslaved Africans in Virginia.
It guaranteed independence from English rule after a set period of time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one of the major characteristics of the House of Burgesses when it was created in 1619?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about who actually got a say in government in 1619.

choices.content: Only landowning white men could vote or serve as representatives.
The assembly replaced the English Parliament as Virginia’s highest authority.
The governor was not a part of the assembly and could not veto laws.
The assembly struggled to include representatives from every English colony.
